identity.canonical_title: Toroidal Field Configuration and Safety Factor

theory.canonical_statement: The optimal gyromagnetic propulsion geometry employs nested toroidal magnetic confinement with toroidal and poloidal components. The safety factor, defined as the ratio of toroidal to poloidal field components times the radial coordinate, must exceed a critical value of approximately 2.8 for stability.
theory.technical_interpretation: The optimal gyromagnetic propulsion geometry employs nested toroidal magnetic confinement with toroidal and poloidal components. The safety factor, defined as the ratio of toroidal to poloidal field components times the radial coordinate, must exceed a critical value of approximately 2.8 for stability.

latex_blocks: Psi(t) \in T^*(SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}^3) = \{r_{cm}, p_{cm}, g, L_{gyro}, \mu_{eff}, A_{obs}\}

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This fixes the toroidal-field safety factor used to diagnose stability of the nested confinement carrier.

formula_latex_blocks: Psi(t) \in T^*(SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}^3) = \{r_{cm}, p_{cm}, g, L_{gyro}, \mu_{eff}, A_{obs}\}
equation_progression.interpretive_role: This identifies the critical threshold required for admissible confinement.
This closes the entry by imposing the stability inequality that the safety factor must satisfy.
